title: AN ACT STANDARDIZING SALARIES OF CERTAIN  OFFICIALS OF THE CITY OF MANILA AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES.

chunks.text:
H. No. 16099 / 65 OG No. 25, 6357 (June 23, 1969)

[ REPUBLIC ACT NO. 5353, June 15, 1968 ]

AN ACT STANDARDIZING SALARIES OF CERTAIN OFFICIALS OF THE CITY OF MANILA AND FOR OTHER PURPOSES.

Be it enacted by the Senate, and House of Representatives of the Philippines in Congress assembled:

SECTION 1. The provisions of the Revised Charter of the City of Manila, as amended, to the contrary notwithstandÂing the following officials of the City of Manila shall receive the annual compensation as fixed hereunder:

Mayor

P36,000.00

Vice-Mayor

30,000.00

Twenty Councilors

26,000.00 each

City Engineer

24,000.00

City Treasurer

24,000.00

City Assessor

24,000.00

City Health Officer

24,000.00

City Auditor

24,000.00

City Public Service Officer

24,000.00

Chief, Manila Fire Department

24,000.00

Superintendent of City Schools

including the National share of P7,200

24,000.00

Chairman, Veterinary Inspection Board

24,000.00

Social Welfare Officer

24,000.00

Secretary to the Mayor

24,000.00

Secretary to the Board

22,000.00

One Assistant Secretary and Administrative Officer of the Board

22,000.00

Assistant City Engineer

22,000.00

Assistant City Treasurer

22,000.00

Assistant City Assessor

22,000.00

Assistant City Health Officer

22,000.00

Assistant City Auditor

22,000.00

Assistant City Public Service Officer

22,000.00

Deputy Chief of Police

22,000.00

Chief Detective Bureau

22,000.00

Deputy Chief, Fire Department

22,000.00

22,000.00

Deputy Chief of Police

22,000.00

Chief Detective Bureau

22,000.00

Deputy Chief, Fire Department

22,000.00

Three Members, Veterinary Inspection Board

22,000.00 each

Two Assistant Superintendents of City Schools

22,000.00 each

Two Assistant Secretaries to the Mayor

22,000.00 each

One Legislative liaison Officer to the Mayor

18,000.00

SEC. 2. The officials herein above mentioned shall reÂceive commutable representation allowance to be determined by the Municipal Board and as approved by the Mayor.

SEC. 3. The incumbent officials holding the positions affected by this Act shall receive the salary increases upon the effectivity thereof without the necessity of new apÂpointments.

SEC. 4. This Act shall take effect upon its approval.

Approved, June 15, 1968.
